puzzles.name: Margaux's Trunk
puzzles.description: An antique trunk with a three-dial combination lock. Each dial shows numbers 0–9. Something precious is locked inside.
clues: Margaux's diary mentions: 'I will lock away what matters most before the final curtain on the 31st.'
Vivian said Margaux always used dates she could never forget.

puzzles.name: The Script Cipher
puzzles.description: Red-circled letters in the margins of 'The Crimson Veil' script. They spell a word when read in order through the stage directions.
clues: Diego noticed the circled letters follow the order of the stage directions, not the dialogue.
The effects manual describes a 'prop goblet' used in Act III for the poisoning scene.
Margaux's diary mentions: 'The tea tastes wrong again. And now the wine in rehearsal...'

puzzles.name: The Lighting Sequence
puzzles.description: The catwalk lighting board. Reproduce the exact lighting cue from the fatal night to reveal what the audience would have seen.
clues: The effects manual lists the Act III lighting sequence: 'crimson wash, moonlight blue, center spot, crimson wash.'
The annotated script has symbols in the margins at Act III: ●○★●
Edwin's notebook maps the symbols: ● = crimson, ○ = moonlight, ★ = spotlight

puzzles.name: The Evidence Board
puzzles.description: Arrange the evidence to connect both mysteries — the 1928 murder of Margaux Fontaine and the modern-day poisoning of Roland Ashworth.
clues: All journal entries and collected items contain pieces of both timelines.
The 1928 timeline: Cecilia obtained poison, switched the prop goblet, Margaux died on stage.
The modern timeline: Edwin discovered the truth, staged the ghost, Ashworth found out, Edwin poisoned him.
